contents.title: Nuxt3: How we eliminated extra 10 API requests on a single page using $fetch and Pinia
contents.description: Discover how we reduced 10 extra API requests in our Nuxt3 app by using $fetch and Pinia. Learn our approach and solutions

children.value: Nuxt3: How We Eliminated 10 Extra API Requests Using $fetch and Pinia
Last year, we upgraded our Nuxt2 app to Nuxt3, which was challenging but worthwhile. Unfortunately, we couldn't fully utilize all the new Nuxt3 features like

because migrating from Axios/Vuex to useFetch/Pinia was time-consuming given our large app. Instead, we went a simpler approach by using $fetch/Pinia, only changing our API plugin wrapper from Axios to $fetch did the job quickly and safelly.
Using $fetch to fetch data and storing it in Pinia Stores
The current way that we're using to handle API requests in our Nuxt3 app is having a wrapper Nuxt plugin called
for making requests, something like this:

props.className: language-js shiki shiki-themes github-light github-dark monokai
language-vue shiki shiki-themes github-light github-dark monokai
props.code:
<template>{{ myData }}</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
const { fetchData, myData } = useContentStore();
await fetchData();
</script>

children.value: Using $fetch causes two requests: one on the server and one on the client. This is by design, as $fetch doesn't transfer data from the server to the client, so it needs to make two requests to ensure the Vue component's data is retrieved again.
Because $fetch does not transfer state from the server to the client. Thus, the fetch will be executed on both sides because the client has to get the data again.
This means if you hit reload on the home page, you will see a newtork request in the devtools for the
API which is extra, as the same exact one happened on the server
Okay, this should be expected to have 2 requests, if you use the
to store data in your Vue component, but it's an issue if you use
to store data in a Pinia store? why? because Pinia state already preserved and transfered from the server to the client (state got hydrated), so that network request is an extra one .
was especially designed to solve such a problem, it's purpose to avoid the extra function executio and it executes once on server, and on client side navigation.
We need to wrap our function call with

props.className: language-vue shiki shiki-themes github-light github-dark monokai
props.code:
<!-- myData is a state propert in our content store -->
<template>{{ myData }}</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
const { fetchData, myData } = useContentStore();
await callOnce(async () => {
  await fetchData();
});
</script>

children.value: <!-- myData is a state propert in our content store -->
Now hit a reload, and watch your network tab, you will no longer see that request. 🔥🔥
This solution fits us, as need something quick and works in the same time, in the meantime, we will be transitiong gradually to other solutions like the below ones.
to fetch data and storing it in a Vue component

props.className: language-js shiki shiki-themes github-light github-dark monokai

children.value: Getting the data and storing it in the component:

props.className: language-vue shiki shiki-themes github-light github-dark monokai
props.code:
<template>{{ myData }}</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
const myData = ref();
const { fetchData } = useContentStore();
const res = await fetchData();
myData.value = res.data;
</script>

children.value: Not all APIs results are stored in Pinia stores, if that was the case, we could just use the above approach. The issue we faced that we have Pinia actions that make APIs requests using
and return the API result to the Vue component and it stored in it, like the above example.
The issue here is that the
executes twice, once on server and once on client. I tried to wrap with

props.className: language-vue shiki shiki-themes github-light github-dark monokai
props.code:
<template>{{ myData }}</template>
<script setup lang="ts">
const myData = ref();
const { fetchData } = useContentStore();
await callOnce(() => {
  const res = await fetchData();
  myData.value = res.data;
});
</script>

children.value: Yes, the above block of code will get executed once, but the data
will be lost from the component when Nuxt hydrates the component.
is a great comosable for handeling APIs easily, you can review it
In our real app we created a wrapper called
so we do it in common things like passing headers, token other stuff. We won't address this point, but if you're intersted check this
is not meant to be used in pinia stores, mainly it should be called in inside
where it can automaticlly stores the API response data into your Vue component, and it makes sure the component's data is transferred from server to client.

props.className: language-vue shiki shiki-themes github-light github-dark monokai
props.code:
<template>{{ myData }}</template>

<script setup>
const { data: myData } = await useFetch("/content/data");
</script>

children.value: Now check your network tab, you won't see the client API request.
